summary: We recommend marking E1 done because the outcome-reviewer subagent successfully ran against the real open PR opensubagents outcomes-mcp 18 scored its outcome+report pair at overall 4.4 against the canonical floor of 3.5 identified clarity as the weakest dimension at score 2 and posted exactly one structured comment in the role-specified shape without modifying the working tree. The tradeoff against running the subagent against a synthetic test PR is that using a real held-for-human-merge PR proves the subagent works in the actual operational state the orchestrator will dispatch it in. The comment URL is preserved as the runroc and the subagent role spec at .claude agents outcome-reviewer md is unchanged.

claims.statement: the outcome-reviewer subagent was dispatched via the Agent tool from this heartbeat tick and followed the documented procedure without modifying any repo files because the subagent read the pair from the feature branch via git show into a temp directory rather than checking out the branch.

citations.title: outcome-reviewer role spec defining the procedure the subagent followed
E1 runroc captures the posted comment author url and body

claims.caveats: The subagent used git show plus temp directory rather than gh pr checkout which is a small departure from step 2 of the role spec; this is an acceptable optimization because it preserves the working tree which the role spec implicitly requires via the step 5 leave the local tree clean constraint.

claims.statement: the subagent scored PR 18 at overall 4.4 against the canonical floor of 3.5 with dimension scores confidence_calibration 5 citation_quality 5 coverage 5 decision_usefulness 5 and clarity 2 because the python HeuristicVerifier returned exactly those numbers and the subagent transcribed them verbatim into the review comment.

citations.title: outcome-reviewer comment on PR 18 with the dimension scores
python HeuristicVerifier that produced the scores

claims.caveats: The clarity score of 2 is because the report summary in tick-17 PR is a single long sentence the heuristic counts as 8 mini-sentences via period splitting; tightening the summary to 2-4 sentences would lift clarity from 2 to 5 and the overall to 5.0 without changing the substance.

claims.statement: the subagent posted exactly one comment on PR 18 with the documented header dimensions weakest-dimension and recommendation fields because the runroc shows a single comment object authored by alex-jadecli with the four sections and the recommendation approve which respects the role constraint never request changes if overall is at or above 3.5.

citations.title: outcome-reviewer comment on PR 18 demonstrating the four-section format
outcome-reviewer role spec section step 4 documenting the comment shape

claims.caveats: The comment was posted under the alex-jadecli alias because that is the gh auth user; if the operator wants subagent comments to be visually distinguishable a future tick should create a dedicated bot account or use a Markdown badge in the comment header.

open_questions: Should E1 be wired into a GitHub Actions workflow that dispatches the subagent on every PR open event rather than relying on the heartbeat to invoke it?
methodology_notes: Dispatched the subagent via the Agent tool with subagent_type general-purpose passing the outcome-reviewer role spec path as the first instruction. The subagent read the role spec then executed gh pr view git show python verify and gh pr comment in sequence. Captured the posted comment as a runroc via gh pr view 18 json comments and saved the result under outcomes runrocs.
